# Overview

This report presents the machine learning evaluation for the UCI 130-US Hospitals
Diabetes dataset. It covers model development, comparison, selection, holdout
evaluation, and deployment implications for a 30-day readmission prediction system.

The analytical work in Part 1 (EDA, causal inference, experimental design) directly
informed feature engineering and modeling choices documented here.

## Dataset

- **Source:** UCI Machine Learning Repository — Diabetes 130-US Hospitals
- **Analytic sample:** 69,581 encounters after deduplication and exclusion criteria
- **Outcome:** 30-day hospital readmission (binary), 7.1% positive rate
- **Period:** 1999–2008 across 130 US hospitals

## Data Splits (temporal via encounter_id percentile)

| Split      | Rows   | Positive rate | Role |
|---|---|---|---|
| Train      | 48,706 | 7.1%          | Model fitting and calibration |
| Validation | 6,958  | 5.6%          | Hyperparameter selection |
| Holdout    | 13,917 | 5.6%          | Final evaluation (tiebreaker) |

## Modeling Pipeline

Four models were developed and compared:

1. **Logistic Regression** — ElasticNet, Optuna 50 trials, Platt calibration
2. **XGBoost** — gradient boosted trees, Optuna 100 trials, Platt calibration
3. **LightGBM** — leaf-wise trees, Optuna 100 trials, Platt calibration
4. **TF/Keras** — MLP [128→64→32], Optuna 25 trials, isotonic calibration

## Analytical Components

1. **Model Comparison** — train/validation performance across all four models
2. **Calibration** — reliability diagrams pre and post calibration
3. **Model Selection** — rationale for productionization choice
4. **Holdout Evaluation** — honest final metrics on the selected model
5. **SHAP Analysis** — feature importance and directionality
6. **Causal Consistency** — SHAP directions vs Part 1 causal estimates
7. **Subgroup Analysis** — performance by diagnosis group, age, admission type
8. **Fairness Assessment** — performance by race and gender
9. **Threshold Sensitivity** — precision/recall/F1 across τ values
10. **Deployment Implications** — key findings for clinical deployment
